name: openbox
description: Approve or reject AI agent actions from OpenBox directly in your editor

openbox.environment.description: [Dev only] Which OpenBox environment the extension talks to. End users should leave this on 'production'. The Switch Environment command is only visible when the extension is running in development mode (Run Extension target). Each env has its own token namespace in ~/.openbox/tokens, so logging in via the CLI to staging won't clobber prod credentials.

openbox.tabObserver.enabled.description: Observe non-keystroke buffer mutations (Tab autocomplete accept, Cmd-K, Composer multi-file edits) and log them to the 'OpenBox: Tab Observer' output channel. Cursor does not expose hooks for these surfaces, so the observer classifies changes heuristically (size + idle timing). Off by default; turn on to inspect what the observer would report before piping to OpenBox.

openbox.tabObserver.outputLog.description: When tabObserver.enabled is true, also write each classified change to the 'OpenBox: Tab Observer' output channel. Set false to suppress that visibility; the in-process classification still runs. Has no effect when tabObserver.enabled is false.

openbox.agentId.description: OpenBox agent ID to evaluate file writes / AI inserts / file ops against. When unset, all active gates (preWriteGate.active, tabObserver.active, fileOpGate.enabled) become no-ops; without an agent ID there is no policy context. The runtime key for this agent must be present in ~/.openbox/agent-keys (populated by `openbox api-key rotate <agentId>`) or as the OPENBOX_API_KEY environment variable visible to the extension host.

openbox.preWriteGate.active.description: When true, every save calls check_governance(file_write) for the configured agent. `allow` lets the save proceed; `require_approval` blocks the save with a modal until decided; `deny` cancels the save with a notification. Network deadline is 4s under VS Code's ~5s save timeout; on timeout or network failure the gate fails open unless openbox.failClosed is set. Requires openbox.agentId.

openbox.tabObserver.active.description: When true (and openbox.tabObserver.enabled is also true), classified non-keystroke inserts are evaluated via check_governance(file_write). Denied inserts get reverted via WorkspaceEdit and a notification is shown. UX is jarring (the insert briefly appears, then disappears); use sparingly and prefer preWriteGate.active for at-save enforcement when possible. Requires openbox.agentId.

openbox.fileOpGate.enabled.description: When true, intercepts onWillCreateFiles / onWillDeleteFiles / onWillRenameFiles and evaluates each via check_governance. Catches Composer multi-file creates and AI-driven file ops that don't route through the agent's Write tool. Requires openbox.agentId.

openbox.failClosed.description: When true, all active gates fail closed (cancel the action) on network errors / timeouts contacting core. Default false (fail open) so a transient network blip doesn't block the user; flip on for high-trust environments where blocking on uncertainty is preferred.
